content.problemStatement:
Little Elephant from Zoo of Lviv likes to paint.


He has **n** objects to paint, ordered in a row and numbered form left to right starting from **1**. There are totally **c** colors, numbered from **0** to **c-1**. At the beggining all objects are colored in color with number **1**. When object with color **a** is painted in color **b**, the resulting color will have number **(a*b) mod c**.


Little Elephant is going to make **k** turns. At **i-th** (0-based numeration) turn he will randomly choose any subset (even empty) of objects with indices in range **[Li; Ri]** (inclusive) and paint all objects in chosen subset with random color (the same for all objects in the subset).


Little Elephant wants to know the expected sum of all colors over all **n** objects after making all **k** turns. Help him.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three integers n, c, and k, where n is the number of objects, c is the number of colors, and k is the number of turns. The next k lines of each test case contain two integers Li and Ri, representing the range of objects that can be painted in the current turn.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the expected sum of all colors over all n objects after making all k turns.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has 4 objects, 3 colors, and 4 turns. The second test case has 7 objects, 10 colors, and 7 turns.
The test case has 5 objects, 5 colors, and 3 turns.
The test case has 10 objects, 10 colors, and 5 turns.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by maintaining the expected color distribution for each object after each turn. For each object, we calculate the expected color distribution after the current turn by considering all possible colors that can be applied to the object in the current turn. We then update the expected color distribution for each object after the current turn.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * k * n * c) per test case, O(T * k * n * c) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n * c) — for storing the expected color distribution for each object.
